url_verification_summary.competitor_section_strategy: session-side WebSearch at write time per §16.31 Wave 8 + Wave 9 5/5 dead-rate pattern + Wave 6/7 §16.43 confirmation. Stage 2 did not invent plausible firm URLs; RUN session sources fresh via Google Search at write time and applies §16.36 statutory-citation cross-check per page. Each pick's competitor section includes 4-6 suggested queries + 4-6 known specialist-firm-class targets (e.g. Blevins Franks, Spence Clarke, Crowe LLP Indian/Spanish desks, BDO ATED desk, Saffery Champness expat).
url_verification_summary.stage2_webfetch_strategy: Stage 2 surfaced authority URLs from Stage 1 seed citations + cross-referenced §16 + §16.5 (state-specific DTA notes) + §17 (Wave 2 expat) + §17.3 (s.10A) + §17.4 (NRCGT) + §17.5 (NRL) + §17.8 (FIG) + §17.9 (TRF) + §17.10 (CGT rebasing election narrow eligibility) + §18 (ATED Wave 3) + §18.1 (2026/27 bands) + §18.5 (ATED-CGT abolition) + §18.6 (RoE-ATED parallel) + §18.7 (OTM campaign) + §21.A (CT 3-figure framework + s.18N(3) expansive exclusion) + §22.X (IHT LTR + tail-period table + Sch A1 enveloped-residential look-through) + §27 (HMRC enquiry + Sch 24 / Sch 55 / Sch 56 + F-5 lock on Sch 24 no-12-month-qualifier + CoP9 vs DDS vs LPC routing) house position locks; per-page legislation.gov.uk + gov.uk + HMRC manual verification deferred to RUN session under §16.35 per-write discipline. F-5 lock (Sch 24 para 10 NO 12-month qualifier on careless-unprompted floor; the 12-month qualifier exists in Sch 41 para 13 only) carried verbatim where relevant (B4 OTM letter brief most directly engages). ITTOIA 2005 s.396B TAAR cite-correctness (per Wave 6 §16.40 lock — NOT ITA 2007 s.396B which does not exist) flagged for B5 ATED pros-cons brief at the de-envelope-via-MVL example.

discoveries_logged: D-1 STAGE2_INHERITED_VERIFICATION — Stage 1 author had already WebFetched + verified 2013 UK-Spain Convention (signature + in-force + UK effective dates + MLI modifications effective from 2023) / 1993 UK-India Convention + 2013 Protocol + MLI from 2020 / 2018 UK-IoM DTA + 2024 MoU + 2021 collection-assistance / FA 2013 Part 3 ss.94-174 ATED architecture / TCGA 1992 s.10A per HMRC CG26540 2026-05-22 / FA 2025 s.37 FIG insertion of ITTOIA 2005 ss.845A-845J / FA 2025 s.41 + Sch 10 TRF / FA 2025 s.42 + Sch 11 CGT rebasing election / IHTA 1984 s.6A LTR + Sch A1 / FA 1995 Sch 23 + SI 1995/2902 NRL / TIOPA 2010 s.18 + s.130 FTC anchors at seed time. Stage 2 statutory-citation cross-check confirmed alignment with §16 + §16.5 (state-specific) + §17 + §17.8 + §17.9 + §17.10 + §18 + §21.A + §22.X locks; no new drift catches at Stage 2 review.

D-2 STAGE2_CONTENT_DEPTH — the 6 Stage 1 seed briefs in this batch are unusually deep at the framing-differentiator + statutory-anchor + key-questions layers (matching M3-A-B1 D-2 + M3-A-B2 D-2 patterns). Each extended brief landed at +200 to +250 lines of additional content (worked examples 5-9 per pick + 12-13 FAQs + universal rules + 19-step workflow + Stage 1 seed work log appendix). This pattern across A-B1, A-B2, and B-B1 confirms Stage 1 quality compounds Stage 2 efficiency.

D-3 STAGE2_TRILATERAL_DTA_DIFFERENTIATION — picks 1 (UK-IoM 2018), 2 (UK-Spain 2013), 3 (UK-India 1993) form a 3-page bilateral-DTA cluster with deliberately distinct framing differentiators: B1 opens with Manx-client-trap (treaty-allocates-not-exempts); B2 opens with two-way credit asymmetry (Spanish wealth tax no-UK-mirror); B3 opens with treaty-silence-is-not-exemption load-bearing (1993 treaty lacks Art 13(4) but NRCGT statutory override applies). Stage 2 anti-templating notes for all three picks explicitly identify the sibling-collapse risk + the structurally-distinct openings + the destination-specific worked examples (IoM: Quayle/Crosby/Cregeen + 2018 modern OECD Art 13(4); Spain: Hernández/Vázquez/wealth-tax/SGF/Brown s.10A + MLI 2023; India: Iyengar/Mehta/indirect-disposal/NRI-OCI/Chowdhury return). RUN session pairing of these three picks should hold the line on per-state distinctness.

D-4 STAGE2_ATED_PAIR_DISCIPLINE — picks 4 (B4 HMRC OTM letters response page) and 5 (B5 ATED pros-and-cons strategic-decision page) form a deliberately adjacent ATED pair: same regime, different audience + different decision-frame. B4 framing is 30-day-triage operational-response for the director who has received an OTM letter; B5 framing is honest-pros-and-cons strategic-decision-frame for the owner contemplating enveloping. Stage 2 anti-templating notes explicitly identify the sibling-collapse risk + the structurally-distinct openings (B4 opens with triage-tree; B5 opens with should-I-envelope). RUN session pairing of these two picks should hold the line on operational-response vs strategic-decision distinctness. Existing site pages (`ated-overseas-companies-voluntary-compliance-otm-letters` for B4; `ated-complete-guide-2026-27` for B5) cross-link bidirectionally — neither new page re-walks the existing ground.

D-5 STAGE2_BUG7_AUTONOMY_CONFIRMED — Bug #7 fix (work autonomously after acknowledgment line) worked cleanly across the 6 picks. No mid-batch pause; no Q-N raised; no blocker. Wall-clock matches the M3-A-B1 D-4 + M3-A-B2 D-5 patterns (~2 hours for 6 substantive brief extensions on main branch with per-pick commit + final batch-done marker).

D-6 STAGE2_CROSS_BUCKET_LTR_CONSISTENCY — picks 2 (UK-Spain), 3 (UK-India), 5 (ATED pros-cons), 6 (Leaving UK permanently) all depend on IHTA 1984 s.6A LTR + tail-period table per §22.X.2 (3-10 year scaling by prior-UK-resident-year count) + Sch A1 enveloped-residential look-through per §22.X.4 (UK situs property in UK IHT regardless of LTR status). The Stage 1 seeds already carry §22.X alignment; Stage 2 brief extensions explicitly teach the LTR mechanics in worked examples (B2 Example 8 Wright 18-prior-years → 8-year tail; B3 Example 8 Vaidyanathan 19-prior-years → 9-year tail; B5 Example 6 Cordero non-LTR but UK property still in IHT; B6 Example 4 Chen/Khan/Roberts 6/13/19 prior-years → no-LTR/3-year-tail/9-year-tail). Site-wide LTR discipline anchor should make the cluster internally consistent at RUN time.

D-7 STAGE2_S10A_CROSS_BUCKET_DISCIPLINE — picks 2 (UK-Spain Brown), 3 (UK-India Chowdhury), 6 (Leaving UK permanently Ellis) all depend on TCGA 1992 s.10A 5-year-or-less + 4-of-7-preceding gateway per §17.3 + HMRC CG26540 verbatim verification anchor. Misframe-grep discipline (Misframe 1: 4 years not 5; Misframe 2/3 across the trio: NRCGT return only-if-tax-due) carried verbatim across all three picks. RUN session GREP at write-time should catch any drift to '4-year temporary non-residence' (a common competitor error).
anti_templating_check: Six briefs read as a set. Boilerplate sections (Voice + style, 19-step workflow, Quality bar) are deliberately verbatim per §4.8 and §7. Substantive content (framing differentiators carried from Stage 1, worked examples, FAQ phrasing, anti-templating notes per pick) is distinct per slug. Each anti-templating note explicitly identifies sibling-collapse risk: B1 UK-IoM (vs Crown Dependencies survey page + B2 + B3); B2 UK-Spain (vs existing opposite-direction UK-Spain page + B1 + B3 + wealth-tax-no-UK-mirror anchor); B3 UK-India (vs existing rental-survey UK-India page + B1 + B2 + treaty-vs-statute indirect-disposal load-bearing); B4 OTM letters (vs existing overseas-entity OTM page + B5 strategic-decision pair + 30-day triage-tree anchor); B5 ATED pros-cons (vs existing ATED complete guide + B4 + B16 error-mechanics + honest-balance pros-cons matrix anchor); B6 Leaving UK permanently (vs existing 12-month checklist + B19 short-term variant + B7 inverse + s.10A 5-year line + LTR tail-table anchor). No same-sentence drift detected across the set; each worked-example persona surname differs per pick (B1: Quayle/Crosby/Kissack/Cregeen/Faragher; B2: Hernández/Vázquez/Romero/Brown/Inversiones Ibérica SL/Castro/Wright; B3: Iyengar/Mehta/Singh/Chowdhury/UK Realty India Private Limited/Reddy/Vaidyanathan; B4: Bridge/Magnolia Investments Limited/Glentworth Holdings BVI/Ortiz/Yew Tree Estates Limited; B5: Singh-Patel/Walker/Sapling Holdings Limited/Ferguson/Cordero; B6: Carter/Ellis/Chen/Khan/Roberts/Marquez/Patel — note: Patel surname appears in B3 (Mehta context) and B6 (Patel rebasing example); distinct persona, distinct narrative, but RUN session should review for any inadvertent surname collisions at write).
